evals.prompt: feat-auth was squash-merged into main. feat-auth-tests (parent: feat-auth) now has duplicate commits from feat-auth in its history. Re-parent feat-auth-tests onto main.
assertions: reads parent_sha_at_stack_time from stack-graph.sh get feat-auth-tests
calls onto-reparent.sh with feat-auth-tests, main, and the recorded SHA
does not call restack.sh or git rebase --update-refs
updates the stack graph to record main as the new parent of feat-auth-tests

evals.prompt: I want to reparent feat-auth-tests onto main. The stack graph records parent_sha_at_stack_time as deadbeef123 but feat-auth-tests has already been rebased and that SHA is no longer reachable from it.
assertions: calls onto-reparent.sh which performs the ancestor check
stops when onto-reparent.sh exits non-zero due to failed ancestor check
reports that the recorded old-base SHA is not reachable and the stack graph needs updating

evals.prompt: A teammate pushed three commits to feat-auth. feat-auth-tests is now behind its parent but feat-auth was NOT squash-merged — the commits are still there on feat-auth. I want to bring feat-auth-tests up to date.
assertions: recognizes this is ordinary divergence, not a squash-merge case
defers to /stack-restack which uses restack.sh and --update-refs
explains that /stack-reparent is for squash-merge duplicate-commit removal, not normal divergence
